questions.text: How does the Surah describe the 'Earthquake of the Hour'?
questions.answer: As a terrible thing that will make every nursing mother forget her child and every pregnant woman miscarry.

questions.text: What are the stages of human creation mentioned in Verse 5?
questions.answer: Dust, then a sperm-drop (Nutfah), then a clinging clot (Alaqah), then a lump of flesh (Mudghah), partly formed and partly unformed.

questions.text: Describe the person who worships Allah 'on the edge' (Verse 11).
questions.answer: If good befalls him, he is content; but if a trial strikes him, he turns on his face, losing both this world and the Hereafter.

questions.text: Does everything in the universe prostrate to Allah?
questions.answer: Yes, including the sun, moon, stars, mountains, trees, animals, and many of mankind.

questions.text: What was the command given to Ibrahim (AS) regarding the Kaaba?
questions.answer: To purify His House for those who perform Tawaf, those who stand in prayer, and those who bow and prostrate.

questions.text: What is the significance of the 'Adhan' for Hajj as mentioned in Verse 27?
questions.answer: Allah commanded Ibrahim to proclaim the Hajj so people would come on foot and on every lean camel from every distant pass.

questions.text: What reaches Allah from the animal sacrifice (Qurbani)?
questions.answer: Neither their meat nor their blood reaches Allah; what reaches Him is the 'Taqwa' (piety) from the hearts.

questions.text: When was the first permission to fight given to the Muslims?
questions.answer: In Verse 39: 'Permission [to fight] has been given to those who are being fought, because they were wronged'.

questions.text: What would happen if Allah did not check one set of people by means of another?
questions.answer: Monasteries, churches, synagogues, and mosques—in which the name of Allah is much mentioned—would have been demolished.

questions.text: How long is a 'Day' with your Lord according to Verse 47?
questions.answer: Like a thousand years of those which you count.

questions.text: What does Allah do with the suggestions Satan throws into the revelations of Prophets?
questions.answer: Allah eliminates what Satan throws in and then makes His verses precise.

questions.text: What is the 'Blindness' described in Verse 46?
questions.answer: It is not the eyes that are blind, but the hearts within the breasts.

questions.text: What is the parable of the 'Fly' mentioned in Verse 73?
questions.answer: If all those called upon besides Allah gathered, they could not create a fly, and if a fly took something from them, they could not recover it.

questions.text: What title did Allah give to the followers of the Prophet (SAW) from before?
questions.answer: Muslims.

questions.text: What is the purpose of being 'witnesses over mankind'?
questions.answer: The Messenger is a witness over the Muslims, and the Muslims are witnesses over mankind.

questions.text: What are the symbols (Sha'a'ir) of Allah?
questions.answer: The rites and places of worship (like Hajj rituals), and honoring them is from the piety of hearts.

questions.text: What happens to the 'wrongdoing towns' that Allah gives respite to?
questions.answer: He seizes them eventually, and to Him is the final return.

questions.text: What is the reward for those who emigrated for the cause of Allah and were then killed or died?
questions.answer: Allah will surely provide for them a good provision.

questions.text: How does Allah describe His power over the night and day in this Surah?
questions.answer: He causes the night to enter the day and the day to enter the night.

questions.text: What are the final commands in the last verse of Surah Al-Hajj?
questions.answer: Bow, prostrate, worship your Lord, and do good—that you may succeed.
